medical_history.history_of_present_illness: The female infant was delivered by vacuum extraction following induction of labor at 37 weeks of gestation. Antenatal ultrasound findings of short limbs, micrognathia, small thoracic dimension and overlapping fingers indicated a skeletal dysplasia. The pregnancy was complicated by polyhydramnios necessitating four amnioreductions during the last six weeks of the pregnancy. At birth, the baby was intubated for respiratory distress and transferred to the neonatal intensive care unit. The baby had short limbs, micrognathia, a large cleft palate, small and low-set ears and short overlapping fingers. A skeletal survey showed a short broad thorax with horizontally aligned ribs, shortened long bones, marked metaphyseal widening of the long bones, rounded hypoplastic vertebral bodies, and delayed ossification of the symphysis pubis. The diagnosis of spondyloepiphyseal dysplasia (SED) congenita was made based on the skeletal survey and clinical picture.

physical_examination.general_examination: At birth, the baby weighed 3075 g (50th percentile), the head circumference was 35.5 cm (50th percentile), and the length 42 cm (53rd percentile). The Apgar scores were 6 and 9 at 1- and 5-minutes respectively. She was intubated for respiratory distress and transferred to the neonatal intensive care unit. The baby had short limbs, micrognathia, a large cleft palate, small and low-set ears and short overlapping fingers.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: A skeletal survey showed a short broad thorax with horizontally aligned ribs, shortened long bones, marked metaphyseal widening of the long bones, rounded hypoplastic vertebral bodies, and delayed ossification of the symphysis pubis. The diagnosis of spondyloepiphyseal dysplasia (SED) congenita was made based on the skeletal survey and clinical picture. Ultrasound examinations of the head, heart, and abdomen were normal.

radiographic_examinations.findings: Short broad thorax with horizontally aligned ribs, shortened long bones, marked metaphyseal widening of the long bones, rounded hypoplastic vertebral bodies, and delayed ossification of the symphysis pubis

Structurally normal heart with a patent ductus arteriosus measuring 4 mm in diameter with bidirectional shunting, minimal tricuspid regurgitation, a time to peak velocity (TPV) to right ventricular ejection time (RVET) ratio of 0.17 and intraventricular septal motion consistent with pulmonary hypertension
radiographic_examinations.changes_over_time: After treatment with sildenafil, TPV/RVET increased to 0.27, tricuspid regurgitation was absent, but some abnormal septal motion persisted

radiographic_examinations.findings: Shortened long bones, metaphyseal changes of the long bones, delayed ossification of the os pubis, horizontal ribs with small thorax, and reduced lung volumes

diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Persistent pulmonary hypertension of the newborn (PPHN) secondary to pulmonary hypoplasia in spondyloepiphyseal dysplasia congenita
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was based on clinical findings of respiratory distress, skeletal abnormalities (short limbs, micrognathia, small thoracic dimension, overlapping fingers), and skeletal survey results showing a short broad thorax with horizontally aligned ribs, shortened long bones, and delayed ossification of the symphysis pubis. Echocardiography confirmed pulmonary hypertension with a structurally normal heart, patent ductus arteriosus with bidirectional shunting, and minimal tricuspid regurgitation.
